_note: JPods QA Document templates — load via manage.py or QAService.create_question_group_from_document()
_format: Each entry becomes a Document record with questions in config, narrative in body

inspections.name: JPods Daily Pre-Op Vehicle Inspection
inspections.description: Daily vehicle inspection before first passenger service — ASTM F770, FTA 49 CFR 673
inspections.body: WHY: ASTM F770 requires daily pre-operation inspection of all amusement rides and devices before opening to patrons. FTA 49 CFR 673.27 requires safety assurance through performance monitoring. A vehicle that passes daily pre-op is cleared for passenger service. A vehicle that fails any RED item is pulled from service until corrected (NCR per QM-13). This inspection takes approximately 10 minutes per vehicle and must be completed by a trained operator (QM-18 qualified).

questions.question: Vehicle exterior: any visible damage, cracks, or deformation?
Doors: open and close fully, latch securely?
Door seals: intact, no gaps?
Interior: seats secure, upholstery intact, floor clean?
Lighting: interior and exterior lights functional?
Cameras: all cameras (forward, rear, merge, interior) producing image?
ToF sensors: forward, rear, merge sensors responding?
Smoke detector: test button confirms alarm?
Emergency communication: mic and speakers functional?
Bogie 1: visual inspection — no loose fasteners, no fluid leaks, wheels intact?
Bogie 2: visual inspection — no loose fasteners, no fluid leaks, wheels intact?
Battery state of charge: above minimum for first trip?
Bumpers: front and rear bumpers secure?
No Smoking signs: visible interior and exterior?
USB ports: functional?
answers: N/A — no ports on this vehicle

questions.question: Climate control: HVAC responding to setpoint?
answers: N/A — outdoor temp within comfort range

questions.question: Low-speed test run: vehicle moves smoothly, no unusual noise or vibration?
Braking test: vehicle stops within expected distance?
Vehicle cleared for passenger service?

inspections.name: JPods Daily Pre-Op Station Inspection
inspections.description: Daily station inspection before first passenger service — ASTM F770, FTA 49 CFR 673
inspections.body: WHY: Every station must be inspected before accepting passengers. The Mind-the-Gap system is safety-critical — a station that cannot verify gap compliance cannot open doors. Platform surfaces, guardrails, gates, lighting, and emergency systems must all be confirmed operational. ASTM F770 requires this daily. FTA 49 CFR 673.27 requires safety assurance monitoring. Station failures are logged as FAULT files and trigger NCR per QM-13.

questions.question: Platform surface: clean, dry, anti-slip intact, no debris?
Guardrails: secure, no damage, no loose connections?
Gates: open and close, interlock functional?
Emergency stop buttons: all functional (test each)?
Lighting: platform >= 50 lux, boarding zone >= 100 lux?
Mind-the-Gap sensors: all door positions responding?
Mind-the-Gap: test vehicle dock — gap within 17mm H / +/-8mm V?
Mind-the-Gap: correction actuator functional?
Elevator: operational, emergency phone working?
Stairs: handrails secure, treads intact, anti-slip?
Tactile paving: intact at platform edges?
Surveillance cameras: all producing image?
Weather protection: canopy intact, drainage clear?
Anemometer: reading plausible, responding to wind?
Station cleared for passenger service?

inspections.name: JPods Weekly Guideway Inspection
inspections.description: Weekly guideway segment inspection — ASTM F770, structural monitoring
inspections.body: WHY: Guideways are the circulatory system. A guideway failure during operation is catastrophic. Weekly visual inspection catches problems that daily vehicle telemetry might miss: corrosion, loose fasteners, settlement, vegetation encroachment, drainage blockage. ASTM F770 requires routine inspections at appropriate intervals. This inspection covers one guideway segment (pier-to-pier). Each segment must be inspected at least weekly.

questions.question: Truss: visual check for cracks, deformation, or corrosion?
Pier/column: plumb, no visible settlement, base plate intact?
Anchor bolts: all present, no visible corrosion, nuts tight?
Footing: no visible cracking, settlement, or erosion around base?
Running surface: smooth, no debris, no foreign objects?
Guideway channel: drainage clear, no standing water?
Expansion joints: functional, not seized or jammed?
answers: N/A — no joints on this segment

questions.question: Solar panels: intact, no damage, connections secure?
answers: N/A — no panels on this segment

questions.question: Position markers: present and readable?
Vegetation: no encroachment within 1m of guideway?
Lighting: guideway-mounted lights functional?
Notes / observations:

inspections.name: JPods Monthly Mind-the-Gap Calibration
inspections.description: Monthly calibration verification of station gap sensors — QM-11, ASCE 21.2-2008
inspections.body: WHY: Gap sensors drift. QM-11 requires calibrated measurement equipment. ASCE 21.2-2008 sets the hard compliance limits (25mm H / +/-12mm V). Our target (17mm / +/-8mm) requires sensor accuracy of +/-1mm. Monthly calibration verification confirms sensors are reading true. Use calibrated gauge blocks at each door position. Record both the gauge reading and the sensor reading. Difference > 1mm = recalibrate. Difference > 2mm = sensor replacement.

questions.question: Station ID and name:
Door position 1 — left sensor: gauge reading (mm) vs sensor reading (mm):
Door position 1 — right sensor: gauge reading vs sensor reading:
Door position 1 — vertical sensor: gauge reading vs sensor reading:
Door position 2 — left sensor: gauge reading vs sensor reading:
Door position 2 — right sensor: gauge reading vs sensor reading:
Door position 2 — vertical sensor: gauge reading vs sensor reading:
Maximum deviation found (mm):
All sensors within +/-1mm tolerance?
Calibration gauge ID and cert date:

inspections.name: JPods Annual Engineering Review
inspections.description: Annual structural and systems engineering review — ASTM F770, ASTM F2974
inspections.body: WHY: ASTM F770 requires annual engineering reviews. ASTM F2974 provides the audit framework. This is the comprehensive review that covers everything the daily/weekly/monthly inspections don't reach: structural fatigue analysis, foundation settlement survey, electrical system integrity, control system software verification, emergency system full-function test, documentation review. Must be performed by or under supervision of a licensed PE. Results feed into the annual management review per QM-01.

questions.question: Structural survey: all piers plumb within tolerance, no settlement > 5mm from baseline?
Fatigue review: any evidence of fatigue cracking in truss members or connections?
Foundation inspection: borings/GPR if settlement detected; compare to original design basis?
Corrosion assessment: steel protection system intact, no section loss?
Electrical system: insulation resistance test, ground fault protection, all circuits?
Control system: software version current, all safety interlocks verified?
Emergency systems: all e-stops tested, evacuation equipment present and serviceable?
Vehicle fleet: review all daily inspection logs for trends — any recurring failures?
Mind-the-Gap: review 12 months of gap trend data — any station showing progressive drift?
Documentation review: all QM records current, retention periods met, training records up to date?
Open NCRs/CARs: all reviewed, none overdue?
Reviewing engineer name and PE license number:
Overall system cleared for continued operation?
answers: No — shutdown required until corrections made

training_quizzes.name: QUIZ-SAFETY-DAILY
training_quizzes.description: Training quiz: daily inspection knowledge — must pass before conducting inspections
training_quizzes.body: WHY: QM-18 requires training before personnel perform quality-affecting work. FTA 49 CFR 673.29 requires safety promotion through training. This quiz verifies that an inspector knows what to check, why each item matters, and what to do when something fails. Passing score: 80%. Must be retaken annually.

questions.question: What standard requires daily pre-operation inspection of amusement rides before opening to patrons?
questions.explanation: ASTM F770 — Standard Practice for Ownership, Operation, Maintenance, and Inspection — requires daily pre-op checks.

questions.question: If a vehicle fails any item on the daily pre-op inspection, what is the correct action?
choices: A) Note it and continue service
B) Pull vehicle from service and file an NCR per QM-13
C) Notify Noelle to reroute around it
D) Schedule repair for next week
questions.explanation: Failed pre-op = vehicle pulled from service immediately. NCR documents the nonconformance for tracking and corrective action.

questions.question: What is the JPods target for Mind-the-Gap horizontal clearance?
questions.explanation: 17mm is the JPods target (1/3 below the ASCE 21.2-2008 maximum of 25mm). 25mm is the hard compliance floor.

questions.question: Why do gap sensors measure with AND without vehicle load?
choices: B) To detect wheel wear and suspension faults by comparing unloaded baseline to loaded measurement
D) To test the correction actuator
questions.explanation: Unloaded baseline drifting over weeks = wheel wear or guideway settlement. Loaded gap exceeding target on one vehicle = suspension fault on that vehicle.

questions.question: When does the vehicle door open at a station?
choices: B) When Sally confirms the vehicle is docked
C) After the loaded gap is verified within limits AND the gate interlock is confirmed
D) When a passenger presses the door button
questions.explanation: The door does not open until: (1) gap is verified under actual load, and (2) gate interlock confirms safe boarding path. This is the safety interlock chain.

questions.question: What is the difference between an NCR and a CAR?
choices: A) They are the same thing
B) NCR documents the nonconformance; CAR investigates root cause and implements corrective action
C) NCR is for vehicles; CAR is for stations
D) NCR is temporary; CAR is permanent
questions.explanation: NCR (QM-13) documents WHAT is wrong and its disposition. CAR (QM-14) investigates WHY it happened and prevents recurrence. An NCR may trigger a CAR if the cause needs investigation.

questions.question: What are the four pillars of the FTA Safety Management System (49 CFR 673)?
choices: B) Safety Policy, Safety Risk Management, Safety Assurance, Safety Promotion
questions.explanation: 49 CFR 673 Subpart D defines four SMS components: Policy (§673.23), Risk Management (§673.25), Assurance (§673.27), and Promotion (§673.29). Daily inspections fall under Safety Assurance.

questions.question: How often must Mind-the-Gap sensors be calibration-verified?
questions.explanation: Monthly calibration verification per QM-11 and SPEC-11. Use calibrated gauge blocks. Deviation > 1mm = recalibrate. Deviation > 2mm = replace sensor.

training_quizzes.name: QUIZ-SPEC-CHASSIS
training_quizzes.body: WHY: Everyone working on or around JPods vehicles must understand the chassis spec. This includes mechanical limits, safety features, and passenger protection systems. Knowing the spec prevents damage, injury, and warranty-voiding modifications. Passing score: 80%.

questions.question: What is the maximum gross mass of a JPods vehicle?
questions.explanation: 750 kg gross (vehicle + payload). Payload capacity is 500 kg.

questions.question: At what sustained wind speed does the JPods network cease operation?
questions.explanation: 45 mph sustained = network shutdown. 90 mph = no permanent deformation (structural design load, not operating limit).

questions.question: How many bogies does each JPods vehicle have, and why?
choices: B) 2 — independent power per bogie for safety redundancy
questions.explanation: Two bogies, each with independent power. If one bogie loses power, the other can bring the vehicle to the nearest station. This is a safety architecture requirement.

questions.question: What acceleration must the chassis withstand without cracking, unlimited cycles?
questions.explanation: 0.4g starts and stops, unlimited fatigue life. This drives the structural design of all chassis-mounted components.

questions.question: What happens when children travel alone in a JPods vehicle?
choices: B) Cameras stay on and parents can view the feed
questions.explanation: Per R-01-032: cameras remain active and parents/guardians have live viewing access. This is a passenger safety and trust requirement.

questions.question: What is the minimum door opening width?
questions.explanation: 1.5m minimum — must accommodate wheelchairs, strollers, and luggage.

training_quizzes.name: QUIZ-NCR-PROCESS
training_quizzes.description: Training quiz: nonconformance and corrective action process — QM-13, QM-14
training_quizzes.body: WHY: Every team member must know how to identify, report, and disposition nonconforming products and services. This is not optional — it's the core quality loop. A team member who doesn't file an NCR when they see a problem is the most dangerous person in the organization. Gordy's quality policy: 'There shall never be an injury of a JPods customer due to JPods negligence, ignorance or lack of quality.' Passing score: 90%.

questions.question: You notice a crack in a pier column during a weekly guideway inspection. What is your FIRST action?
choices: A) Take a photo and email it to Bill
B) Segregate the area (block the segment) and write an NCR immediately
C) Monitor it for another week to see if it grows
questions.explanation: QM-13: Originator identifies and segregates FIRST, then completes NCR fields 1-8. Segregation prevents the nonconforming item from being used. NCR documents it. A crack in a structural member is a potential RED — stop-everything issue.

questions.question: What are the six possible dispositions for a nonconforming item per QM-13?
choices: A) Fix, scrap, return, ignore, defer, waive
B) Use as is, return to supplier, scrap, rework, repair, regrade
C) Accept, reject, defer, escalate, investigate, close
D) Pass, fail, conditional, hold, release, destroy
questions.explanation: QM-13 defines exactly six: use as is, return to supplier, scrap, rework, repair, regrade. 'Use as is' and 'repair' require a Deviation/Waiver per QM-13.1.

questions.question: What triggers a CAR (Corrective Action Request)?
answers: A) Every NCR automatically generates a CAR
B) Only when the root cause of a nonconformance needs investigation to prevent recurrence
C) Only the Quality Manager can issue a CAR
D) CARs are only for customer complaints
questions.explanation: Not every NCR needs a CAR. A CAR is warranted when the nonconformance suggests a systemic problem that could recur. The CAR investigates root cause and implements corrective/preventive action.

questions.question: When is a Deviation/Waiver (D/W) required per QM-13.1?
choices: A) Whenever an NCR is filed
B) When the disposition is 'use as is' or 'repair' and it affects coordination, life expectancy, interface, function, or safety
D) Only when the customer requests it
questions.explanation: D/W is required for 'use as is' or 'repair' dispositions that affect: coordination (external requirements), life expectancy, interface, function, or safety. The D/W has an approval chain: Responsible Manager -> Program/Project Manager -> Code Q Director -> Customer.

questions.question: A colleague tells you 'don't write that up, it's a small thing.' What do you do?
choices: B) Write the NCR anyway — the quality system depends on honest reporting
D) Take a photo and wait
questions.explanation: Gordy's quality policy is clear: 'never an injury due to negligence, ignorance, or lack of quality.' Small things become big things. The NCR is the system's immune response — suppressing it is how organizations fail.
